entries.title: Gmail Attachment Cloud Saver
entries.notes: Scrapes attachment metadata from Gmail threads and uploads files to Google Drive or Dropbox in one click. Built with Gmail API + background fetch. This fills the gap between email attachment workflow and cloud storage that no free tool addresses cleanly.
entries.pain: Users lose email attachments when Gmail cleans them out or accounts get deleted.

entries.title: CSP Header Validator
entries.notes: Parses Content-Security-Policy headers on any page, highlights violations, and suggests directive fixes. Built via declarativeNetRequest + content script analysis. DevTools already show violations but lack policy editing—this fills that gap.
entries.pain: CSP misconfigs silently break functionality and are hard to diagnose manually.

entries.title: Third-Party Script License Reporter
entries.notes: Scans script tags, fetches package manifests from CDN-hosted libraries, and flags license conflicts. Uses a lightweight rules engine for common licenses. License compliance scanning exists in build pipelines but not in-browser during development.
entries.pain: Unknown third-party scripts may introduce GPL or copyleft licenses that create legal risk.

entries.title: API Request Mirroring Proxy
entries.notes: Intercepts fetch/XHR, duplicates requests to a user-configured staging endpoint with optional header rewrites. Built as a service worker proxy layer. Similar to tools like Runscope but browser-native and zero-config for internal debugging.
entries.pain: No easy way to mirror production API traffic to staging for realistic debugging.

entries.title: Notion Page Exporter
entries.notes: Adds export buttons to Notion pages for PDF, Markdown, or HTML with proper styling preservation. Uses Notion's internal API or page scraping. Notion's built-in export is clunky; this is a one-click overlay.
entries.pain: Notion pages are siloed and hard to export in clean formats for external sharing.

entries.title: RegEx Live Validator
entries.notes: Runs user-provided regex against all visible page text, highlights matches, and shows capture groups inline. Lightweight—no external dependencies. Existing tools like regex101 require copy-pasting; this runs in context.
entries.pain: Regex patterns are tedious to test against real page content without a separate sandbox.

entries.title: SaaS Keyboard Shortcut Overlay
entries.notes: Detects active SaaS tool via URL patterns and displays a floating overlay of available shortcuts. Community-contributed shortcut database updated via manifest. Works for GitHub, Gmail, Jira, Linear, Figma, and more.
entries.pain: Power users forget shortcuts and waste time navigating menus in tools they use daily.

entries.title: Google Meet Transcript Saver
entries.notes: Captures Meet's live caption stream using accessibility APIs and saves timestamps alongside text. No recording—just captions with timestamps. Meets real privacy constraints while solving a genuine pain point.
entries.pain: Meeting transcripts in Google Meet disappear after sessions end with no easy way to save them.

entries.title: AI Training Data Detector
entries.notes: Maintains a community-curated database of sites known to use user content for model training. Shows a subtle badge on affected pages. Privacy consciousness is rising and no tool surfaces this information at point of browsing.
entries.pain: Users have no visibility into which sites train AI models on their submitted content.

entries.title: Cron Job Health Monitor
entries.notes: Parses public status pages and cron service dashboards to detect missed or delayed executions, showing a badge in the toolbar. Uses lightweight polling. No native browser integration for Cron monitoring exists.
entries.pain: External Cron services like cron-job.org have no browser-level alerting beyond email.

entries.title: Cookie Consent Weaver
entries.notes: Builds a graph of cookie domains encountered and shows how tracking IDs correlate across visits. Visual representation of cross-site tracking chains. Educational privacy tool filling a gap left by consent managers.
entries.pain: Users cannot easily visualize how cookies link their browsing history across unrelated sites.

entries.title: Clipboard Secret Guard
entries.notes: Monitors clipboard content for patterns resembling secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) and warns before paste completes. Uses regex heuristics for common formats. Simple, immediate security win for developers.
entries.pain: Accidentally pasting API keys or passwords into chat or browser triggers security breaches.

entries.title: LinkedIn Company Employee Counter
entries.notes: Scrapes employee count from LinkedIn company pages and auto-populates a CRM field or shows a tooltip. Targets salespeople using LinkedIn Sales Navigator. Headcount is a key signal with no clean browser-native extraction tool.
entries.pain: Sales teams manually check LinkedIn for headcount data to qualify accounts.

entries.title: DOM Mutation Event Replay
entries.notes: Instruments DOM MutationObserver to capture user interactions and serializes them as a replayable JSON trace. No external service, no recording uploads. Lightweight alternative to full session replay tools for quick debugging.
entries.pain: Reproducing UI bugs requires recording sessions through heavy tools like FullStory.

entries.title: Session Share for Async Code Review
entries.notes: Serializes current tab state (DOM, localStorage, console logs) into a shareable link or QR code. Recipient opens link in a sandboxed tab. Useful for reviewing layout bugs or state-dependent issues without scheduling a call.
entries.pain: Sharing a browser state for async code review requires screenshots or screen share recordings.
